prerequisites: A DocuSeal account (cloud at console.docuseal.com, or a self-hosted instance).
A DocuSeal API key generated from the DocuSeal console (the value for the required api_key config field).
A start_date value to mark the earliest point from which to sync records (required start_date config field).
Optionally, a limit value to cap the number of records returned per request (optional limit config field).

authMethods.name: API Key (X-Auth-Token header)
authMethods.summary: DocuSeal authenticates each request with an API key passed in the X-Auth-Token HTTP header (e.g. curl https://api.docuseal.com/templates -H 'X-Auth-Token: API_KEY'). Supply this key as the connector's api_key field. The same value works against the US endpoint (api.docuseal.com) or the EU endpoint (api.docuseal.eu) depending on your account region.

setupSteps.title: Sign in to the DocuSeal console
setupSteps.body: Log in to your DocuSeal account at console.docuseal.com (or your self-hosted DocuSeal instance). Create an account first if you do not have one.

setupSteps.title: Obtain your API key
setupSteps.body: Open the API section of the DocuSeal console and copy the API key. DocuSeal uses this key for token-based authentication via the X-Auth-Token header on every API request.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied key into the connector's required api_key field. This is stored as a secret and sent as the X-Auth-Token header on each call to the DocuSeal API.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should begin retrieving records.

setupSteps.title: Optionally tune the page limit
setupSteps.body: Optionally set the limit field to control how many records are requested per page. Leave it unset to use the API default.
